key_insight: HACO uses sparse human guidance to transfer a masked generative modeling idea from vision into crystal structure prediction, yielding a new CSP model called MaskGXT.
engineering_relevance: The work suggests AI co-scientists can accelerate materials algorithm discovery by combining cross-domain model search with targeted domain constraints to improve crystal generation performance.
method: Searches across generative modeling methods from other fields to find transferable ideas
Instantiates MaskGIT as a discrete token model for crystal structures
Adds symmetry tokens, space-group-stratified sampling, and sub-bin coordinate refinement to form MaskGXT
evidence_signal: On the MP-20 polymorph split, MaskGXT achieves 79.06% METRe accuracy versus 70.87% for the strongest baseline, and it also reports the best match rate on MP-20 and MPTS-52.
watch_item: Results depend on benchmark-specific validation and a carefully designed human steering process, so transferability to broader CSP settings may require additional testing.
paper_title: Discovering Crystal Structure Prediction Algorithms with an AI Co-Scientist
